summary: Verified the two-click inline confirm UX replacing window.confirm() in Admin dashboard. All three affected controls (question delete, level delete, user reset) work correctly: first click puts the button into red 'CONFIRM/Confirm' state without firing the API; second click within 4s triggers the actual mutation; clicking a different pending button resets the previous one; auto-clear after 4 seconds prevents accidental deletes.
passed: Question delete (data-testid=delete-q-1-3): first click shows red bg + 'CONFIRM' label, question NOT removed
Question delete: second click within 4s deletes via DELETE /api/admin/levels/{id}/questions/{qid}, 'Question deleted' toast appears, row count goes from 4 -> 3, TEST DELETE ME prompt removed from DOM

Level delete (data-testid=delete-level-99): first click shows red bg + 'CONFIRM' label, level still present; second click deletes via DELETE /api/admin/levels/{id}, 'Level deleted' toast appears, admin-level-99 disappears
User reset (data-testid=reset-user-test-admin-1): first click shows red bg + 'Confirm' label, second click triggers POST /api/admin/users/{id}/reset, 'Admin Tester's progress reset' toast visible
Switching pending confirm: clicking delete-q-1-0 while delete-q-1-3 was pending reset the first button (text empty/Trash icon) and moved CONFIRM to the new button -- only one pending at a time
Auto-clear after 5s of inactivity: pending CONFIRM state cleared back to normal trash icon, no accidental deletion (row count remained 4 before manual delete)
critical_code_review_comments: Admin.js is 350 lines and mixes 4 components (Admin/LevelsTab/UsersTab/FinalTab/QuestionEditor) -- still under 700-line threshold but consider splitting per tab once feature grows.
deleteQuestion/deleteLvl/reset duplicate the same 'two-click + 4s timeout' pattern -- could be extracted into a useConfirmAction(key, onConfirm) hook for DRY.

setTimeout closures rely on `cur === key` check -- correct, but if the same key is re-clicked the prior timer is NOT cancelled. Harmless because it just no-ops, but a useRef-tracked timer with clearTimeout would be cleaner.
test_credentials: Cookie session_token=test_session_admin_1 for user_id=test-admin-1 (is_admin=true). Confirmed in /app/memory/test_credentials.md format.
seed_data_creation: Created throwaway question (prompt='TEST DELETE ME', answer='delete', order=99) in Level 1 (lvl_08122a4c) -- DELETED during test. Created throwaway Level 99 (lvl_e450bf42, title='TEST LEVEL DELETE ME') -- DELETED during test. Reset test-admin-1's own progress as part of users-tab reset test (score 0, level 1).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Two-click confirm UX is implemented in /app/frontend/src/pages/Admin.js using confirmDel (questions, key=`${levelId}:${questionId}`), confirmLvlDel (levels, key=level_id) and confirmReset (users, key=user_id) state. 4-second auto-clear via setTimeout with stale-check via setState callback. All three flows verified end-to-end against live backend with real toasts and DOM verification.
